stories.title: Firm Named After Russia Sanctions Breach Settlement
stories.summary: Petrofac Facilities Management Limited has paid HMRC a compound settlement of £569,157.07 for offences under The Russia (Sanctions) regulations, with HMRC publicly naming the firm as part of its enforcement action.

stories.title: Government Steps Up Drive to Reconnect Young People with £1.6 Billion in Unclaimed Child Trust Fund Savings
stories.summary: A new taskforce has been set up by the government to encourage young people to claim their Child Trust Funds, with an estimated £1.6 billion remaining unclaimed across millions of accounts.

stories.title: Asylum Seekers to Pay £10,000 Towards Living Costs Under New UK Law
stories.summary: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has announced a new means-tested scheme requiring asylum seekers to repay around £10,000 for their state-funded accommodation and support once they begin earning, or risk being denied settled status.

stories.title: Inside Shabana Mahmood's New UK Asylum Reforms
stories.summary: The Home Secretary set out further planned reforms to the UK asylum system, including the £10,000 repayment scheme and other structural changes aimed at reducing costs and tightening the immigration and asylum bill.

stories.title: Residents 'Shocked' at MoD Bicester Asylum Seeker Plan
stories.summary: Residents living near a site proposed to house more than 1,000 asylum seekers at MoD Bicester say they are shocked by the Home Office announcement, raising concerns about safety and lack of community consultation.

stories.title: New UK Envoy to Champion LGBT+ Rights Globally
stories.summary: The UK government has appointed Lord Collins as a new special envoy to champion LGBT+ rights globally, with a mandate to strengthen international partnerships to tackle discrimination, violence and persecution.

stories.title: FCDO Updates Venezuela Travel Advice
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office has updated its travel advice for Venezuela, advising against all travel to parts of the country due to ongoing safety and security risks.

stories.title: Youth Jobs Grant Guidance Published
stories.summary: The Department for Work and Pensions published new guidance for employers on applying for grant funding to deliver the Youth Jobs Grant, aimed at supporting young people into employment.

stories.title: Child Maintenance Service Statistics: Data to March 2026
stories.summary: DWP released the latest Child Maintenance Service statistics showing a 5 percentage point increase in paying parents making some maintenance payments between the quarters ending March 2025 and September 2025.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Industrial Emissions and Waste Transfer Data Published
stories.summary: DEFRA published the latest annual datasets for industrial emissions and waste transfer (UK PRTR data), providing updated environmental reporting figures for public access.

stories.title: Rolls-Royce PLC Environmental Permitting Direction Issued
stories.summary: DEFRA issued an exercise of functions direction to Rolls-Royce PLC under the Environmental Permitting (England and Wales) Regulations, setting out regulatory requirements for the company.

stories.title: Introducing Sustainability into Service Assessments
stories.summary: DEFRA Digital published learnings from introducing sustainability criteria into service assessments, as part of its 'Delivery Groups' initiative coordinating services around areas such as farming and circular economy.

stories.title: Starmer Unveils 'Hybrid Navy' and £5bn for Drones in Long-Delayed Defence Plan
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer set out a long-delayed defence investment plan committing billions of pounds to the armed forces, including transforming the Royal Navy into a 'Hybrid Navy' combining autonomous vessels and AI with traditional warships, and a £5bn investment in drones.

stories.title: Army Recruitment Fell by 40% in 2025, MoD Confirms
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence confirmed that the number of people applying to enlist in the regular army fell by around 40% in 2025, raising concerns about the future size and readiness of UK armed forces.

stories.title: Navy Plans Reflect Sea Change in How Wars Are Fought
stories.summary: BBC Security Correspondent Frank Gardner analyses the new defence investment plan, noting it reflects rapid and fundamental changes in drone warfare and the evolving nature of modern conflict.

stories.title: UK's New Steel Trade Measure Takes Effect from 1 July 2026
stories.summary: From 1 July 2026, the government is limiting tariff-free steel imports, reducing overall quota volumes by 51% compared with the previous steel safeguard measure, following an announcement on 19 March 2026.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT Publishes Equality Objectives for 2026 to 2030
stories.summary: The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology published its equality, diversity and inclusion objectives for 2026–2030, focusing on tackling digital exclusion, empowering people to thrive, and designing user-centred services.

stories.title: England to Get Powerful Maternity Commissioner After 'Shocking Failings'
stories.summary: The Health Secretary has announced a new independent maternity commissioner for England following the Amos Review, which found childbirth and neonatal care to be in urgent need of reform after shocking failings across NHS trusts.

stories.title: Resident Doctors in England Accept Government Offer on Pay and Jobs
stories.summary: Resident doctors (formerly known as junior doctors) in England have accepted a government offer on pay and job conditions, marking a significant resolution to an ongoing dispute that had impacted NHS services.
